title: Codonocarpus pyramidalis
intro: Codonocarpus pyramidalis, also known as the Australian finger lime, is a small, succulent tree native to the eastern coast of Australia, characterized by its unique, finger-shaped citrus fruits. The health benefits of Codonocarpus pyramidalis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and limonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Codonocarpus pyramidalis is often made into a tea or juice, or used as a topical application to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Codonocarpus pyramidalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Surianaceae, commonly known as the desmond green or ironwood tree. It is native to the western part of Australia. This plant belongs to the genus Codonocarpus, which contains two to three species of small to medium-sized trees. The Surianaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to Australia, and it is closely related to the family Fabaceae. Codonocarpus pyramidalis is also sometimes referred to as a member of the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants.
